title: Use search_code with pattern "def\s+\w+\(.*\):" to find func…

messages_history.content:
Use search_code with pattern "def\s+\w+\(.*\):" to find function definitions.
Does regex work correctly?

The regex pattern `def\s+\w+\(.*\):` successfully found function definitions in the Python code. Here are some of the matches:

- In `tests/test_example.py`:
  - `def test_greet_basic(self):` at line 20
  - `def test_greet_empty_name(self):` at line 24
  - `def test_add_positive(self):` at line 36
  - `def test_multiply_positive(self):` at line 48
  - `def test_initial_value(self):` at line 64
  - `def test_reverse(self):` at line 127

The regex works correctly for identifying function definitions in the provided Python files. If you need further analysis or modifications, let me know!
